name: hdl-wave-ai
description: AI-assisted hardware verification using VCD and FST waveforms with local or cloud LLMs. Supports Claude, NVIDIA TRT-LLM (DGX Spark), Ollama, vLLM, and any OpenAI-compatible API. Features tool-use (RAG) mode with 10 waveform query tools and a standalone MCP server.

commands.title: HDL Wave AI: Analyze Waveform File…
HDL Wave AI: Debug VaporView State

enumDescriptions: Use any OpenAI-compatible API (Ollama, NVIDIA NIM, vLLM, etc.)
hdlWaveAi.provider.description: LLM provider to use for analysis

hdlWaveAi.openaiCompatible.baseUrl.description: Base URL for OpenAI-compatible API (default is Ollama)

hdlWaveAi.openaiCompatible.apiKey.description: API key for OpenAI-compatible API (any string works for Ollama)

hdlWaveAi.openaiCompatible.useCompletion.description: Use the /v1/completions endpoint instead of /v1/chat/completions. Enable this for TRT-LLM models where the chat endpoint is broken (e.g. Qwen3, Nemotron). Formats prompts as ChatML and parses tool calls from text output.

hdlWaveAi.waveform.defaultEndTime.description: Fallback simulation end time when no VaporView markers are set. Increase for longer simulations without markers.

hdlWaveAi.waveform.sampleStepSize.description: Time step size for waveform sampling (in simulation time units)

hdlWaveAi.waveform.maxTransitions.description: Maximum number of signal transitions to send to the LLM. If the collected transitions exceed this, they are evenly sampled to preserve temporal spread. Increase for larger models with bigger context windows.

hdlWaveAi.waveform.useToolMode.description: Use tool-calling (RAG) mode for waveform analysis. The LLM queries signal data on-demand via tools instead of receiving all transitions upfront. Requires a provider that supports function calling (OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic). Falls back to legacy mode if unsupported.

hdlWaveAi.hdl.searchPaths.description: Additional absolute directory paths to search for HDL source files (e.g. your RTL directory outside the workspace)

hdlWaveAi.hdl.maxModules.description: Maximum number of HDL modules to send to the LLM, ranked by relevance to tracked signals

hdlWaveAi.hdl.maxCharsPerModule.description: Maximum characters per module. Larger modules are truncated at this limit.

hdlWaveAi.chat.maxHistory.description: Maximum number of chat messages to keep in context when conversational mode is enabled.

hdlWaveAi.chat.conversational.description: When true (default), prior exchanges are included up to maxHistory. When false, each query is stateless — exactly 2 messages sent per inquiry (system + user).

hdlWaveAi.prompt.maxTokens.description: Maximum estimated prompt tokens before truncation kicks in. Set below your model's context limit to leave room for output tokens and tool-call overhead. For TRT-LLM with max_num_tokens=32768, use 28000.

hdlWaveAi.toolLoop.maxRounds.description: Maximum tool-call rounds before forcing a final answer. 0 (default) = auto-calculate from prompt.maxTokens (roughly maxTokens/2000, clamped 5-30). Increase for deeper analysis with larger context models.

hdlWaveAi.isa.description: Instruction set architecture for decoding instruction bus values in waveform analysis. Set this to match your design's CPU core.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
